solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int countEven(int num) {
        int count = 0;
        for (int i = 1; i <= num; i++) {
            int sum = 0;
            int n = i;
            while (n > 0) {
                sum += n % 10;
                n /= 10;
            }
            if (sum % 2 == 0) {
                count++;
            }
        }
        return count;
    }
}
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(N * D), where N is num and D is the number of digits in the largest number (at most 4 for num <= 1000)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution iterates through all numbers from 1 to num, calculates the digit sum for each, and counts how many have even digit sums. It is efficient for small constraints like num <= 1000. The main weakness is that it recalculates digit sums for each number, which could be optimized with precomputation for larger constraints.>
